get.description:
Retrieves a list of currently available models.

The response format is automatically identified based on request headers:
- Returns Anthropic format when https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D and `anthropic-version` headers are present
- Returns Gemini format when `x-goog-api-key` header or `key` query parameter is present
- Returns OpenAI format otherwise
Returns the list of available models in Gemini API format

post.description:
Creates a model response based on conversation history. Supports both streaming and non-streaming responses.

Compatible with OpenAI Chat Completions API.

OpenAI Responses API, used to create model responses.
Supports multi-turn conversations, tool calling, reasoning, and more.

Gemini image generation.

The Nano Banana series supports OpenAI Dall-E format (see link [Image Generation/OpenAI Dall-E Format](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D) for details).
Image generation interface in OpenAI Dall-E format.
OpenAI Dall-E format image editing interface.

OpenAI Sora compatible format video generation interface. Supports text-to-video and image/video reference generation modes.

- `POST /v1/videos`: Create video task
- `GET /v1/videos/{video_id}`: Query task status
- `GET /v1/videos/{video_id}/content`: Get video binary content (download)

Standard Authentication Header:
- `Content-Type: application/json` (When creating task)

1. Call `POST /v1/videos` to create a task and obtain `id`.
2. Poll `GET /v1/videos/{id}` until `status=completed` or `failed`.
3. After `completed`, prioritize using `video_url` from the response; if unified gateway download is required, call `GET /v1/videos/{id}/content`.

## 3.1 Core Parameters

- `model: string` (Required)
- Description: The name of the video model to be called.
- Example: `sora-2`, `doubao-seedance-2-0-260128`

- `prompt: string` (Recommended)
- Description: Main description for video generation. Must be provided even when using `content`, cannot be empty.
- Example: `第一人称视角果茶广告，节奏轻快`

- `seconds: number|string` (Optional)
- Description: Target duration. Mutually exclusive with `duration`; if both are provided, the gateway parsing result usually prevails.
- Example: `8`

- `size: string` (Optional)
- Description: Output resolution, commonly `宽x高`.
- Example: `720x1280`, `1280x720`

- `mode: string` (Optional)
- Description: Generation mode, defaults to `t2v` if not provided. Different modes have different requirements for asset parameters.
- Example: `t2v`, `i2v`, `i2v_first_last`, `reference_material`

## 3.2 General Compatibility Parameters

- `aspect_ratio` or `ratio`: Aspect ratio.
- `duration`: Duration alias (mutually exclusive with `seconds`).
- `quality` or `resolution`: Definition-related parameters.
- `fps`: Frame rate.
- `function_mode`: Optional feature mode, used to trigger specific behaviors (e.g., `omni_reference` denotes Omni-Reference semantics).

## 3.3 Reference Asset Parameters

- `input_reference`: Standard reference input object.
- Description: Only supports string URL format; object format is not supported.
- Correct Example: `"input_reference": "https://example.com/ref.png"`
- Incorrect Example: `"input_reference": { "image_url": "https://example.com/ref.png" }`

- `image_url` / `image_urls`: Image reference.
- Description: `image_url` is commonly used for single images; `image_urls` is used for multiple images or Omni-Reference.

- `end_image_url` / `last_image_url`: Last-frame reference.
- Description: Usually paired with `mode=i2v_first_last` to represent the final frame of the video.

- `video_urls`: Video reference list.
- Description: Commonly used in `reference_material` or `content` Omni-Reference scenarios.

- `audio_urls`: Audio reference list.
- Description: Used for background music/voiceover style reference (effectiveness depends on model capability).

- `reference_images`: Multi-image reference.
- Description: Commonly used in multi-frame/multi-image reference modes.

Note: Different models have different requirements for asset fields; it is recommended to pass parameters according to the "Mode and Minimum Parameters" section below.

## 3.4 content Advanced Mode (Recommended for complex reference scenarios)

You can directly pass an array of `content`:
- `type=text`: Text instruction.
- `type=image_url|video_url|audio_url`: Media input.
- Supports `name` for referring to assets in text via `@名称`.

Required Constraints:
- When using `content`, `prompt` must still be provided and cannot be empty.

Compatible Syntax:
- `image_url/video_url/audio_url` can be a string or an object `{ "url": "https://..." }`.
- `role` can use `material`, and is also compatible with `reference_image/reference_video/reference_audio`.

Can be carried simultaneously:
- `generate_audio: boolean`
- `watermark: boolean`

Additional Notes:
- `generate_audio=true`: Request the model to generate audio simultaneously (only effective for models with this capability).
- `watermark=false`: Request to disable watermark (actual effectiveness depends on model and channel policies).
- `name`: Asset alias, recommended to match `@名称` in `text` for accurate asset association by the model.

Parameter support varies significantly across model families; refer to the table below for selection:

| Model Family | Common Model Name (Example) | Recommended Min. Parameters | Common Extension Parameters | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Seedance | `doubao-seedance-*` | `model + prompt + mode` | `image_url/image_urls/end_image_url/reference_images/video_urls/audio_urls/function_mode/content` | Most sensitive to `mode`; not all versions support `reference_material`. |
| Sora General | `sora-2` `sora-2-pro` and `*-stable/*-official` | `model + prompt` | `seconds/size`, `input_reference` (string URL) recommended | Image requirements may vary during multi-channel fallback; preparing reference images is advised to improve success rate. |
| Sora Duration Preset | `sora2-landscape-10s` `sora2-pro-portrait-25s` etc. | `model + prompt` | `image_url/input_reference` | Duration and orientation are usually determined by the model name; `seconds/ratio` is only effective if allowed by the model. |
| Veo | `veo3*` `veo3.1*` | `model + prompt` | `mode + image_url/reference_images/content + ratio/seconds` | `i2v/r2v` class models usually require reference images or assets; `t2v` class usually support pure text. |
| Kling | `kling-video` `kling-*` | `model + prompt + mode` | `mode=t2v/i2v/multi_i2v`, and official fields like `image/image_list` | Compatible with official path semantics (text2video/image2video/multi-image2video) and unified into `/v1/videos`; video extension capability not provided externally. |
| Aggregated Extension Models | `happyhorse*` `grok-imagine-1.0-video*` etc. | `model + prompt` | `seconds/size/content` | Supported fields depend on model and channel implementation; prioritize standard fields, using `content` only if necessary. |

Notes:
1. If you use stable alias models (e.g., `*-stable`, `*-official`), the underlying channel may switch automatically; please try to pass "Cross-channel compatible parameters" (`prompt + model + seconds/duration + size/ratio + 必要参考图`).
2. For complex multi-asset scenarios (image+video+audio+@name), prioritize using `content` for highest compatibility.
3. If model documentation does not explicitly support a field, first run with "Minimum Parameters" before gradually adding extension parameters.

### 4.1 Kling Official Mode Supplement

- `mode=t2v`: Text-to-Video
- `mode=i2v`: Image-to-Video (Requires `image` or `image_url`)
- `mode=multi_i2v`: Multi-image Reference (Requires `image_list` or multiple `image_urls`)
- `mode=extend`: Not supported (Video extension `/v1/videos` capability currently not provided externally)

Common official parameters can be passed through directly in the `/v1/videos` request body:
- `model_name` `negative_prompt` `cfg_scale` `mode(std/pro)` `duration` `aspect_ratio`
- `sound` `camera_control` `callback_url` `external_task_id` `watermark_info`

### 4.2 Grok Video Mode Supplement

Recommended Parameters:
- `model`: e.g., `grok-video-3`
- `prompt`
- `ratio` or `aspect_ratio`
- `resolution` or `size`
- `seconds` or `duration`
- `image` / `image_url` / `input_reference` (Single-image reference)
- `reference_images` / `reference_image_urls` (Multi-image reference, official `reference-to-video` semantics)
- `mode` (Optional): `image-to-video`, `reference-to-video`

Constraints:
- `mode=edit-video` / `mode=extend-video` not supported (Video editing and extension currently not available externally).
- `image/input_reference` and `reference_images` cannot be passed simultaneously.

- `mode=t2v`
- Min. Parameters: `model + prompt`

- `mode=i2v` (Equivalent to `i2v_first`)
- Min. Parameters: `model + prompt + image_url` (or `input_reference` string URL)

- `mode=i2v_first_last`
- Min. Parameters: `model + prompt + image_url + end_image_url`

- `mode=reference_images`
- Min. Parameters: `model + prompt + reference_images` (Usually 1–4 images)

- `mode=reference_material`
- Min. Parameters: `model + prompt + (image_urls/video_urls/audio_urls 至少一种)`
- `content + function_mode=omni_reference` is highly recommended for complex scenarios.
- Note: When `function_mode=omni_reference` is passed, it will be processed with Omni-Reference semantics even if `mode=reference_material` is not explicitly passed.

images.description: Unified multi-image entry. Used by Vidu, Seedance, Veo, etc.

seconds.description: Target duration. Mutually exclusive with `duration`; if both are provided, the gateway parsing result usually prevails. Example: `8`

OpenAI Compatible video task status query interface.

Returns detailed status information of the video task.

video_url.description: Video result URL. Different models may return either https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D or `url`.

url.description: Video result URL. Different models may return either https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D or `video_url`.

This endpoint returns the video file stream via proxy.

Generate videos from text descriptions using Kling models.

Supported models: kling-v1, kling-v1-5, etc.

post.description:
This series supports OpenAI video generation formats (see [Video Generation/Sora Compatible Formats](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_32%5D%5D) for details).

Used to create video generation tasks and query status and results via Task ID.

Core fields for task creation:
- https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D: Required. Model name.
- `prompt`: Required. Main description for video generation.
- `content`: Optional. Multimedia input array in advanced mode.
- `function_mode`: Optional. Often used for advanced modes like universal reference.

Compatible input parameters:
- `mode`: `t2v / i2v / i2v_first_last / reference_images / reference_material`
- `image_url / image_urls / input_reference`
- `end_image_url / last_image_url`
- `video_urls / audio_urls`
- `seconds / duration`
- `aspect_ratio / ratio / size`
- `quality / resolution`
- `fps`
- `generate_audio / watermark`

Invocation details:
- When `mode=reference_material` or `function_mode=omni_reference` is used, it is recommended to pass text and reference materials via `content`.
- Media fields in `content` support direct URL strings as well as object notation, e.g., `{ "url": "https://example.com/ref.mp4" }`.
- `ratio`, `duration`, `resolution`, and `fps` will affect the final generation configuration.

Key fields in query results:
- `items[].status`
- `items[].content.video_url` or `items[].video_url`
- `items[].error`
- `items[].progress`

Error fields: `error.code`, `error.message`.

``json
{
"error": {
"code": "invalid_request_error",
"message": "requires at least one image in `images`"
}
}
``

prompt.example: 参考素材生成一段剧情短视频
prompt.description: Required. Main description for video generation; cannot be empty.

content.description: Optional. Multimedia input array in advanced mode; recommended for complex reference scenarios.

function_mode.description: Optional. Advanced function mode, such as `omni_reference` for versatile reference.

mode.description: Optional. Compatibility mode fields, such as `t2v`, `i2v`, `i2v_first_last`, `reference_images`, `reference_material`.

image_url.description: Optional. URL for a single reference image.

image_urls.description: Optional. List of URLs for multiple reference images.

end_image_url.description: Optional. URL for the end-frame reference image.

last_image_url.description: Optional. Alias for the end-frame reference image URL.

video_urls.description: Optional. List of reference video URLs.

audio_urls.description: Optional. List of reference audio URLs.

seconds.description: Optional. Video duration in seconds; compatible with `duration`.

duration.description: Optional. Video duration in seconds; compatible with `seconds`.

ratio.description: Optional. Alias for aspect ratio, such as `16:9`.

size.description: Optional. Compatibility field for resolution or dimensions.

watermark.description: Optional. Whether to add a watermark.

example.prompt: 参考素材生成一段剧情短视频

content.text: @角色A 在咖啡馆和 @角色B 聊天，@背景视频 作为场景，配上 @背景音乐 --ratio 16:9 --dur 10

This series supports OpenAI video generation formats (see [Video Generation/Sora Compatible Formats](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_32%5D%5D) for details).

Used to create video generation tasks and query status and results via Task ID.

Core fields for task creation:
- https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_0%5D%5D: Required. Model name.
- `prompt`: Required. Main description for video generation.
- `content`: Optional. Multimedia input array in advanced mode.
- `function_mode`: Optional. Often used for advanced modes like universal reference.

Compatible input parameters:
- `mode`: `t2v / i2v / i2v_first_last / reference_images / reference_material`
- `image_url / image_urls / input_reference`
- `end_image_url / last_image_url`
- `video_urls / audio_urls`
- `seconds / duration`
- `aspect_ratio / ratio / size`
- `quality / resolution`
- `fps`
- `generate_audio / watermark`

Invocation details:
- When `mode=reference_material` or `function_mode=omni_reference` is used, it is recommended to pass text and reference materials via `content`.
- Media fields in `content` support direct URL strings as well as object notation, e.g., `{ "url": "https://example.com/ref.mp4" }`.
- `ratio`, `duration`, `resolution`, and `fps` will affect the final generation configuration.

Key fields in query results:
- `items[].status`
- `items[].content.video_url` or `items[].video_url`
- `items[].error`
- `items[].progress`

Error fields: `error.code`, `error.message`.

``json
{
"error": {
"code": "invalid_request_error",
"message": "requires at least one image in `images`"
}
}
``

post.description:
This series supports OpenAI video generation formats (see link [Video Generation/Sora Compatible Formats](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_3%5D%5D) for details).

This interface topic page is only intended to explain the Veo native format and current gateway support status.

For the current Veo series, please use the external OpenAI Sora compatibility mode: `POST /v1/videos`.

The Google native mode path is reserved as: `POST /v1beta/models/{model}:predictLongRunning`. The current gateway has not yet connected to the Veo native task distribution link, so calling via the native https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_2%5D%5D request body is currently not supported.

prompt.example: A cinematic aerial shot of a modern city at sunrise

referenceImages.description: Optional. 1–3 reference images, using Google native `inlineData` or file URI structures.

instances.prompt: A cinematic aerial shot of a modern city at sunrise

200.description: Reserved success response structure. Currently for protocol description only; actual calls are not supported.

name.description: operation name, used for subsequent status queries.

get.description:
This series supports OpenAI video generation formats (see link [Video Generation/Sora Compatible Formats](https://www.google.com/search?q=%5B%5BKEEP_2%5D%5D) for details).

This interface topic page is only intended to explain the Veo native query protocol.

For the current Veo series, please use the `/v1/videos` unified interface for creation and queries instead.

The following `operation_name` query structure is kept for reserved protocol description purposes only and does not support actual calls.

parameters.name: operation_name
parameters.description: The full operation name returned by the creation interface. `/` must be URL-encoded when called.

200.description: Reserved query response structure. Currently for protocol description only; actual calls are not supported.

name.description: Operation name, consistent with the `name` returned by the creation interface.

response.description: The result object after successful completion.

uri.description: The video URL after successful generation.

tool_call_id.description: Tool Call ID (for tool role messages)

parameters.description: Parameter definition in JSON Schema format

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort (for models that support reasoning)

input.description: Input content, can be a string or an array of messages
Input content, can be a string or an array of messages

metadata.description: Extended parameters (e.g., negative_prompt, style, quality_level, etc.)

remixed_from_video_id.description: Source video ID (if generated based on another video)

BearerAuth.description:
Use Bearer Token authentication.
Format: `Authorization: Bearer sk-xxxxxx`

SessionAuth.name: session
SessionAuth.description: Session authentication, obtained via login interface

AccessToken.name: Authorization
AccessToken.description: Access Token authentication, format: Bearer {access_token}, generated via /api/user/token interface

NewApiUser.name: New-Api-User
NewApiUser.description: User ID request header; must match the current logged-in user ID and is required when using Session or Access Token authentication
